front.title: EMILIA Protocol: Grid Curtailment Authorization Profile (Proof-of-Curtailment)

abstract.t: This document defines grid.curtailment, an EMILIA Protocol (EP) action-type profile for authorizing and proving bounded, reversible curtailment of electrical load by autonomous or agentic systems -- the "Proof-of-Curtailment" used by the GRACE (Grid-Responsive Authorized Compute Events) vertical. It rides the EP authorization receipt () and the human-oversight profile, and introduces no new cryptography. A market-authorized party issues a bounded curtailment order that a facility verifies offline and fail-closed; the event emits a settlement-grade, offline-verifiable bundle proving who authorized it, what was allowed, whether the facility complied, and what should be paid. EP proves authorization and evidence integrity -- a necessary, not sufficient, condition; it does not invent the demand-response baseline.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: Demand-response markets pay large flexible loads (notably AI datacenters) to curtail, but the record that a load actually curtailed when paid is self-reported and trust-based. This profile supplies a portable, tamper-evident, offline-verifiable artifact for that record. It does not move power (a scheduler does) and does not define the baseline methodology (the program/ISO does); it binds the authorizing party to the exact, bounded, reversible event and makes the application of the program's prescribed baseline method un-fudgeable.

section.name: The Curtailment Order
Gate Predicates (Fail-Closed)
section.t: A facility controller changes posture only if all hold: the order verifies (Ed25519 over RFC 8785 / JCS, ) against the pinned authority key; action_type is "grid.curtailment"; the current time is within window; and the current time is before expires_at. Otherwise the action is refused and no posture change occurs.

section.name: Telemetry Attestation and the Proof-of-Curtailment Bundle
section.t: Power telemetry is signed by an attested meter (an EP-RECEIPT-v1 over {meter_id, unit, baseline_method_hash, samples}), so any altered sample breaks verification. The Proof-of-Curtailment Bundle composes the order, the facility acknowledgment, the attested telemetry, and the computed delivered kW*h, with the pinning keys. Verification (all MUST pass): the order verifies against the authority key; the acknowledgment against the facility key; the telemetry against the meter key; telemetry.baseline_method_hash equals order.baseline_method_hash; and recomputing delivered kW*h from the signed samples equals the claimed value. Anyone can run this offline, with no account.

section.name: Relationship to Other Work
section.t: This is a profile of ; human oversight rides the EP human-oversight profile; long-term preservation rides EP Evidence-Record renewal (-style); optional transparency anchoring composes with SCITT/COSE. It composes with -- does not replace -- energy-market dispatch protocols (for example OpenADR, IEEE 2030.5), which carry the dispatch while EP carries the authorization and the proof.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Over-trust is the dominant risk: a valid bundle proves authorization and telemetry integrity against a pinned method, not that the baseline is physically correct (baseline estimation belongs to the program). Spoofed, stale, and replayed orders are refused fail-closed. EP is a necessary, not sufficient, condition for trustworthy demand response.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document registers the "grid.curtailment" action-type in the EP action-type profile registry. It has no other IANA actions.
